title: sphaeralcea grossulariifolia subsp. pedata
intro: Sphaeralcea grossulariifolia subsp. pedata, commonly known as the desert marigold or pedate globe mallow, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is known for its bright yellow and orange flowers, which bloom from March to July, and has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The roots and flowers of Sphaeralcea grossulariifolia subsp. pedata have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antihistamine properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, such as eczema and acne.
